rationale: The cited paper is used in the core conceptual framing of the article, not just in a background list. Osinga et al. cite Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002) to support their key distinction that short- and long-term effects are still transient when they assume mean reversion, contrasted with truly persistent effects. That distinction is central to the citing paper's hypotheses and model motivation. Although the citing paper does not directly reuse the cited paper's empirical setting, it relies on it as a conceptual anchor in persistence modeling.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper to ground its persistent-versus-transient effect framework, especially the mean-reversion interpretation of transient effects. This framing supports the motivation for developing a new time-varying persistence model.
